name: Jetpack Compose ScrollCapture support
platform: AndroidX Compose UI on Android; the long-screenshot path requires Android 12+ / API 31+ for platform ScrollCapture
version: 1.11.2 (latest stable as of 2026); support introduced in 1.7.0
project_status: Actively maintained in AndroidX Compose UI; latest stable 1.11.2 released 2026-05-19; long-screenshot support was introduced in 1.7.0
capture_driver: AndroidComposeView participates in platform scroll-capture search, discovers scrollable Compose semantics, and serves native long-screenshot requests through Compose scroll-capture internals
capture_method: ScrollCapture API via Compose UI integration
compose_support: Experimental/limited support introduced in Compose UI 1.7+ for Compose scroll containers such as LazyColumn and other vertical scroll containers on API 31+
view_support: Compose scroll containers inside AndroidComposeView; interoperability with the surrounding View hierarchy depends on the host window
stitching_strategy: Native coordinate/tile capture through ScrollCapture; no manual bitmap seam matching
matching_algorithm: None; Compose maps requested regions to scroll semantics and rendered output
scroll_offset_trust: Semantic scroll position plus requested capture coordinates, not pixel-feature matching
duplicate_frame_handling: Generally avoided because the system requests distinct regions rather than comparing repeated screenshots
confidence_fallback: Search may return no target when support is unavailable; unsupported cases fall back to a normal screenshot path
static_chrome_handling: Targets the actual Compose scrollable region rather than unrelated window chrome
dynamic_content_robustness: Better than manual screen stitching, but live recomposition, animations, and lazy-loaded content can still affect consistency if content changes mid-capture
content_type_fitness: Good for Compose lists and articles rendered in a single scroll container; weaker for exotic custom layouts or unsupported semantics
memory_strategy: Incremental ScrollCapture rendering to Surface with Compose-managed drawing, avoiding full giant offscreen composition where possible
stitching_speed: Usually efficient on supported containers because it uses native scroll capture instead of CV stitching
max_output_constraints: [uncertain] Limited by platform ScrollCapture/session buffers, device memory, and host screenshot export limits
output_format: Rendered image tiles or bitmaps; final saved image format is chosen by the screenshot host
output_quality: High native render quality; final compression depends on host export
min_sdk: 21 overall for Compose UI; 31 for scroll-capture support
oem_compatibility: [uncertain] Depends on OEM support for Android scroll-capture UX and behavior
integration: androidx.compose.ui:ui; support is built into AndroidComposeView on API 31+ and relies on Compose semantics plus platform ScrollCapture
media_projection_compliance: Not applicable; this feature uses ScrollCapture, not MediaProjection
windowing_behavior: Captures the Compose scroll target within the app window, not the full display
permissions_required: No special permission beyond the normal screenshot flow
chart_stitching_fitness: [uncertain] Good for long charts when rendered in a supported Compose scroll container, but exact coverage for custom drawing or sticky elements is still experimental
chat_stitching_fitness: [uncertain] Good for LazyColumn-style chat UIs on supported versions, but sticky headers and some edge cases were explicitly incomplete in early 1.7 implementation
